title: geum hispidum subsp. ceretanum
intro: Geum hispidum subsp. ceretanum, also known as Ceretan Avens, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing anxiety and improving digestion, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Geum hispidum subsp. ceretanum can be prepared as a tea or infusion, often combined with other herbs, and is also used in the production of tinctures and essential oils for medicinal purposes.
